stem: By interpreting the integral as the area of a familiar region, evaluate \[ \int_{-[[r]]}^{[[r]]} \sqrt{[[rsq]] - x^{2}}\,dx. \]
solution: The graph of \( y = \sqrt{[[rsq]] - x^{2}} \) is the upper half of the circle \( x^{2}+y^{2}=[[rsq]] \) of radius \( [[r]] \). The integral is the area of that semicircle, \( \tfrac{1}{2}\pi([[r]])^{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the area of the whole circle, not the semicircle.
This is a quarter of the circle's area.
This uses \( \tfrac13 \) of the circle's area with no geometric basis.
This treats the region as a square of side \( [[r]] \) doubled.
This is just \( r^{2} \), forgetting the factor \( \tfrac{\pi}{2} \).

stem: By interpreting the integral as the area of a familiar region, evaluate \[ \int_{0}^{[[r]]} [[k]]x\,dx. \]
solution: The graph of \( y=[[k]]x \) from \( x=0 \) to \( x=[[r]] \) bounds a right triangle with legs \( [[r]] \) and \( [[k]]\cdot[[r]] \). The integral is its area, \( \tfrac{1}{2}[[r]]([[k]]\cdot[[r]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This treats the region as a full rectangle instead of a triangle, forgetting the factor \( \tfrac12 \).
This is just the height \( [[k]]\cdot[[r]] \) at \( x=[[r]] \), not the triangle's area.
This drops the coefficient \( [[k]] \) from the height.
This doubles the correct area.
This is off by one from the correct area.

stem: By interpreting the integral as the area of a familiar region, evaluate \[ \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]} \left([[m]]x + [[c]]\right)dx. \]
solution: The graph of \( y=[[m]]x+[[c]] \) is a line staying positive on \( [[[a]],[[b]]] \), so the region under it is a trapezoid with parallel sides \( [[m]]\cdot[[a]]+[[c]] \) and \( [[m]]\cdot[[b]]+[[c]] \) and width \( [[b]]-[[a]] \). The integral is its area, \( \tfrac{1}{2}\bigl(([[m]]\cdot[[a]]+[[c]])+([[m]]\cdot[[b]]+[[c]])\bigr)([[b]]-[[a]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses only the left height, treating the region as a rectangle.
This uses only the right height, treating the region as a rectangle.
This forgets the factor \( \tfrac12 \) in the trapezoid area formula.
This doubles the correct area.
This is off by one from the correct area.

stem: By interpreting the integral as the area of a familiar region, evaluate \[ \int_{-[[W]]}^{[[W]]} [[k]]\left([[W]] - |x|\right) dx. \]
solution: The graph of \( y=[[k]]([[W]]-|x|) \) is an isosceles triangle with base \( 2\cdot[[W]] \) and height \( [[k]]\cdot[[W]] \) (its apex at \( x=0 \)). The integral is its area, \( \tfrac{1}{2}(2\cdot[[W]])([[k]]\cdot[[W]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This doubles the correct area.
This halves the correct area.
This uses only the base of the triangle, dropping the height and the \( \tfrac12 \) factor.
This drops a factor of \( [[W]] \) from the height.
This is off by \( [[k]] \) from the correct area.

stem: By interpreting the integral as the area of a familiar region, evaluate \[ \int_{0}^{[[r]]} \sqrt{[[rsq]] - x^{2}}\,dx. \]
solution: The graph of \( y = \sqrt{[[rsq]] - x^{2}} \) is the upper half of the circle \( x^{2}+y^{2}=[[rsq]] \) of radius \( [[r]] \), and \( 0 \le x \le [[r]] \) picks out exactly one quarter of that circle. The integral is the area of that quarter circle, \( \tfrac{1}{4}\pi([[r]])^{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the area of the full semicircle (from \( -r \) to \( r \)), not this quarter.
This is the area of the whole circle.
This is one eighth of the circle's area.
This is just \( r^{2} \), with no factor of \( \pi \).
This double-counts \( r^{2} \) with no geometric basis.
